title: Griddled Mexican Cod Tacos
description: Tangy and fluffy cod tacos grilled to a perfect sear, served with fresh citrus and aromatic spices for a quick weeknight dinner.
instructions: Heat a tava-griddle over medium-high heat and brush with a thin layer of olive oil.
Season the cod fillets with salt, pepper, garlic powder, smoked paprika, and cumin, then drizzle with lime juice and fish sauce.
Place the seasoned fillets on the hot griddle and cook for 3 minutes on each side until golden and opaque.
Transfer the cooked cod to a cutting board, let rest for 2 minutes, then flake into bite-size pieces with a fork.
Warm the corn tortillas on the griddle for 20 seconds per side to soften them.
Divide the flaked cod evenly among the tortillas.
Top each taco with a squeeze of fresh lime juice and a sprinkle of chopped cilantro if desired.
Serve immediately with a side of sliced avocado or a simple cabbage slaw for extra crunch.
Enjoy the tangy, fluffy flavors of this quick Mexican cod taco recipe.
